stories.body: The United States extended its air campaign against Iran into a ninth consecutive night after confirming a third American service member’s death in recent days, with President Donald Trump saying the latest attacks were carried out in honor of the fallen troops as Brent crude prices climbed above $90 per barrel; the strikes hit command centers, air defenses, missile and drone launch sites, and coastal surveillance assets from Iran’s Gulf ports to the northwestern city of Tabriz, while Iran’s Revolutionary Guard reported retaliatory missile and drone attacks on U.S. positions in Jordan and Kuwait and tensions flared across the Strait of Hormuz where shipping traffic has slowed dramatically.
stories.headline: US presses ninth night of strikes on Iran as Strait of Hormuz crisis escalates

stories.body: Thousands of supporters of the viral Cockroach Janata Party attempted to march on India’s parliament as the monsoon session opened, defying a police ban imposed amid outrage over leaked national exam papers, hunger strikes by activists, and demands for the education minister’s resignation, marking one of the largest shows of dissent in central Delhi in years despite tighter policing and restrictions on public gatherings.
stories.headline: India faces rare mass protest near parliament over exam scandal

stories.body: At least 67 of the 133 people aboard the MV Barima have been rescued after the 1939-built ferry capsized late Saturday en route from Georgetown to Port Kaituma, prompting a massive search-and-rescue effort across more than 1,000 square kilometers while officials investigate an inaccurate passenger manifest and reports that the captain and first engineer tested positive for cannabis, with two deaths confirmed and dozens still missing.
stories.headline: Rescuers search for survivors after Guyana ferry disaster

stories.body: A 5.5-magnitude earthquake struck near Sicaya in Peru’s Huancayo province late Saturday, killing at least six people, injuring more than 30, displacing 300 residents, and collapsing adobe buildings including a church and convent, with authorities still assessing the number of missing amid frequent aftershocks in the seismically active Andes region.
stories.headline: Magnitude-5.5 quake kills six in Peru’s Andes

stories.body: More than 4.2 million U.S. borrowers fell into default between April 2025 and March 2026 after pandemic-era payment pauses and a one-year grace period ended, pushing the total to roughly 9.5 million—about one in five federal borrowers—as the Trump administration dismantles the SAVE income-driven repayment plan, raising fears of wage and Social Security garnishments once collections resume and highlighting acute stress in Southern states with the highest default rates.
stories.headline: Student loan defaults surge as protections expire

stories.body: Maine Senate President Troy Jackson secured commitments from more than two-thirds of the state’s 601 Democratic convention delegates after sweeping county-level caucuses and prompting rivals Shenna Bellows and Nirav Shah to exit, positioning the progressive logger to formally claim the nomination next weekend and pivot into a compressed general-election fight against Republican Sen. Susan Collins, who holds an $11 million war chest.
stories.headline: Troy Jackson all but clinches Maine Democrats’ Senate nod

stories.body: Following widespread reports of fans duped by “ghost tickets” at the World Cup, legislatures in Illinois, North Carolina, Washington, D.C., Connecticut, Oregon, Vermont and others have passed or advanced bans on speculative ticket listings, while advocates split over federal fixes such as the bipartisan TICKET Act that cleared the Senate Commerce Committee but is criticized by venue groups for loopholes that could still allow sellers to advertise tickets they do not possess.
stories.headline: States move to ban speculative ticket resales after World Cup fiascos

stories.body: At the Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator’s Road to 2028 summit, local leaders touted investments in electric buses, tree planting and clean waterways to make the 2028 Olympics a climate success, but community and transit advocates warned that structural budget deficits at LA Metro and Metrolink, coupled with limited federal support under the Trump administration, threaten long-term service and the lasting benefits of pre-Games spending.
stories.headline: Los Angeles weighs climate legacy of 2028 Olympics amid budget strain

stories.body: Fare network executive director Piara Powar urged FIFA not to follow the International Olympic Committee’s newly adopted blanket ban on transgender women in female categories, warning that a similar policy sought by the Trump administration as it negotiates hosting the 2031 Women’s World Cup would stigmatize transgender players and women with differences in sex development even though there is no evidence of transgender athletes nearing elite soccer selection.
stories.headline: FIFA anti-discrimination partner opposes proposed transgender ban

stories.body: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez traveled from New York to Algiers for meetings with President Abdelmadjid Tebboune and Prime Minister Sifi Ghrieb aimed at finalizing the thaw in relations strained by Madrid’s 2022 support for Morocco’s Sahara autonomy plan and at negotiating increased pipeline and LNG supplies from Spain’s top gas provider as the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted.
stories.headline: Sánchez visits Algeria to cement rapprochement and secure more gas

stories.body: After a bruising term marked by high-profile court setbacks, the Sánchez government is negotiating a housing decree with investiture allies and plans to approve new pharmaceutical and tobacco legislation at the July 28 cabinet meeting, the last before the summer recess and the date of the prime minister’s end-of-session address, in a bid to regain political momentum.
stories.headline: Spanish government races to pass housing decree and health laws

stories.body: An uncontrolled blaze that ignited Thursday near La Mierla in Guadalajara’s Sierra Norte has scorched roughly 16,000 hectares within a 100-kilometer perimeter, doubling its spread rate to 4 km per hour amid strong winds, prompting the evacuation of about 20 towns, confinement orders in others, and deployment of more than 500 firefighters and 25 aircraft focused on protecting population centers as the fire advances toward Alto Rey.
stories.headline: Wildfire in Guadalajara burns 16,000 hectares and forces mass evacuations

stories.body: Catalan President Salvador Illa accepted the resignation of Social Rights Minister Mònica Martínez Bravo for personal reasons and promoted department secretary general Raúl Moreno, a social educator and longtime PSC lawmaker, to continue reforms such as the overhaul of the child-protection agency and the Pla Cura initiative to speed dependency benefits.
stories.headline: Raúl Moreno named Catalonia’s new social rights minister

stories.body: A ground collapse on Carrer Teodora Lamadrid in Sant Gervasi has left eight buildings evacuated and residents worried that the L9 metro tunnel boring machine could damage more homes, with neighbors reporting cracked tiles and walls, daily monitoring by topographers, and uncertainty over whether insurers will cover future incidents as the excavation continues beneath the neighborhood.
stories.headline: Sant Gervasi sinkhole heightens fears over L9 tunneling

stories.body: Barcelona’s city hall launched the “Barcelona at 50°C” project under its 2025-2035 heat plan to stress-test infrastructure and emergency protocols for future extreme heat, warning that prolonged heat waves could trigger cascading failures in electricity, communications and water supplies, while meteorological data show the Raval station has already logged 48 tropical nights and a record 23 torrid nights this year, underscoring the urgency of long-term adaptation.
stories.headline: Barcelona plans extreme-heat drill as tropical nights set records
